certs.name: CHFI
certs.description: Entry-level digital forensics certification. Covers evidence collection, file system forensics, mobile forensics, and report writing. Good starting point for forensics career path.

certs.name: EnCE
certs.description: Intermediate forensics certification focused on EnCase platform. Covers evidence acquisition, analysis, and reporting using industry-standard tools.

certs.name: GCFE
certs.description: Advanced Windows forensics certification. Covers Windows artifact analysis, registry forensics, user activity reconstruction, and timeline analysis. Requires SANS FOR500 training (~$8,000).

certs.name: GCFA
certs.description: Advanced incident response and forensics certification. Covers memory forensics, timeline analysis, threat hunting, and enterprise-scale investigations. Requires SANS FOR508 training (~$8,000).

certs.name: CFCE
certs.description: Expert-level forensics certification from International Association of Computer Investigative Specialists. Rigorous practical exam covering evidence handling, analysis, and courtroom testimony.

certs.name: ECIH
certs.description: Entry-level incident response certification. Covers incident handling methodology, evidence collection, containment strategies, and post-incident activities.

certs.name: GCIH
certs.description: Advanced incident response certification. Covers detection, analysis, containment, and remediation of security incidents. Includes hands-on labs with real-world scenarios. Requires SANS SEC504 training (~$8,000).

certs.name: GSNA
certs.description: Network-focused incident response certification. Covers network traffic analysis, packet capture, protocol analysis, and network forensics. Requires SANS SEC503 training (~$8,000).

certs.name: BTL2
certs.description: Advanced blue team certification. Covers threat hunting, SIEM correlation, incident response automation, and advanced log analysis. Practical exam with live environment.

certs.name: MTIA
certs.description: Threat intelligence certification. Covers intelligence lifecycle, OSINT collection, threat actor profiling, indicator analysis, and intelligence reporting.

certs.name: eJPT
certs.description: Entry-level hands-on penetration testing certification. 48-hour practical exam covering network attacks, web exploitation, and pivoting. Great first pentest cert.

certs.name: Pentest+
certs.description: Entry-level penetration testing certification. Covers planning, information gathering, vulnerability assessment, penetration testing, and reporting. Mix of multiple-choice and performance-based questions.

certs.name: BTL1
certs.description: Intermediate blue team certification with offensive components. Covers SIEM, log analysis, incident response, and basic penetration testing for defenders. Practical exam.

certs.name: OSCP
certs.description: Industry-standard penetration testing certification. 24-hour practical exam covering network exploitation, Active Directory attacks, web attacks, and privilege escalation. Requires PEN-200 course (~$1,500).

certs.name: GPEN
certs.description: Advanced penetration testing certification. Covers reconnaissance, exploitation, post-exploitation, and reporting. Includes legal and compliance considerations. Requires SANS SEC560 training (~$8,000).

certs.name: OSWE
certs.description: Expert-level web application security certification. 48-hour practical exam covering source code review, white-box testing, and advanced web exploitation. Requires WEB-300 course (~$1,500). OSCP recommended prerequisite.

certs.name: OSEP
certs.description: Expert-level adversary simulation certification. 48-hour practical exam covering advanced evasion, lateral movement, and custom tooling. Requires PEN-300 course (~$1,500). OSCP required prerequisite.

certs.name: GREM
certs.description: Expert-level malware analysis certification. Covers static/dynamic analysis, debugging, disassembly, and malware behavior extraction. Requires strong programming knowledge. Requires SANS FOR610 training (~$8,000).

certs.name: OSEE
certs.description: Expert-level exploit development certification. Covers advanced Windows exploitation, kernel debugging, bypass techniques, and custom shellcode development. Requires EXP-401 course (~$1,500).

certs.name: OSCE3
certs.description: Elite certification combining OSEP, OSWE, and OSED. Demonstrates mastery across penetration testing, web exploitation, and exploit development. Requires passing all three exams.
